title: Earnings week ahead: NVDA, CRM, HD, BIDU, LOW, DELL, SNOW, AMC, ZM, and more
content:
[earnings]
MCCAIG

The last week of February brings another packed stretch of earnings, with a mix of megacap technology leaders, consumer bellwethers, energy producers, financial firms, and high-growth disruptors set to report.

The lineup spans AI heavyweights, real estate income plays, cloud software providers, and cyclical industrial names, offering investors a broad read on enterprise spending, housing-linked demand, digital advertising, and commodity trends as the quarter progresses.

Technology and AI will dominate the spotlight with updates from NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]), Salesforce (CRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM]), Snowflake (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]), The Trade Desk (TTD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TTD]), Zoom Communications (ZM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZM]), Zscaler (ZS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZS]), C3.ai [https://C3.ai] (AI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AI]), IonQ (IONQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IONQ]), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QBTS]), Dell Technologies (DELL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DELL]), HP (HPQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPQ]), Workday (WDAY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDAY]), Intuit (INTU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTU]), CoreWeave (CRWV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWV]), and SoundHound AI (SOUN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOUN]).

Consumer, retail, and payments trends will be reflected through results from The Home Depot (HD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD]), Lowe's (LOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LOW]), Block (XYZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XYZ]), MercadoLibre (MELI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MELI]), Baidu (BIDU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU]), Lucid Group (LCID [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LCID]), AMC Entertainment (AMC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMC]), Hims & Hers Health (HIMS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HIMS]), and Teladoc Health (TDOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TDOC]).

Energy, infrastructure, and materials updates from ONEOK (OKE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKE]), Dominion Energy (D [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D]), Cheniere Energy (LNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LNG]), Diamondback Energy (FANG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FANG]), EOG Resources (EOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EOG]), MP Materials (MP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MP]), and First Solar (FSLR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FSLR]) will offer signals on commodity demand, pricing, and capital discipline.

Real estate, income, and financially linked names, including Realty Income (O [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/O]), VICI Properties (VICI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VICI]), American Tower (AMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMT]), Innovative Industrial Properties (IIPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IIPR]), EPR Properties (EPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EPR]), Main Street Capital (MAIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAIN]), and Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TD]), will add insight into financing conditions, leasing demand, and credit trends.

Altogether, the week’s reports should provide a wide-angle view on AI spending momentum, consumer resilience, capital investment, and interest-rate sensitivity as markets head toward the final stretch of the quarter.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of February 23 to February 27:

MONDAY, FEBRUARY 23

DOMINION ENERGY (D [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D])

Dominion Energy (D [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D]) is set to report Q4 earnings before Monday’s market open, with analysts expecting roughly a 16% Y/Y increase in profits on revenue growth of more than 9%.

The utility was recently in focus after securing a court ruling [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4539989-dominion-wins-court-ok-to-resume-virginia-offshore-wind-project-stopped-by-trump] allowing it to resume work on its $11.2B Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project. A U.S. District Court granted a preliminary injunction preventing the federal government from enforcing a stop-work order after the company argued the halt was causing irreparable financial harm. Dominion has already invested nearly $9B in the project, which is expected to generate enough power for about 600K homes and remains central to its long-term growth strategy.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating and sell-side consensus both stand at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D/ratings/quant-ratings].

However, SA Investing Group Leader The Value Portfolio rates the stock a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4867876-dominion-energy-coastal-wind-moving-forward-with-a-strong-portfolio], citing Dominion’s roughly $50B capital investment plan, a dividend yield near 4.3%, and a long-term EPS growth target of about 6%. With the offshore wind project now about two-thirds complete and data-center-driven electricity demand rising, the company expects stronger cash flow and continued shareholder returns, though project risks and potential equity dilution remain key watch points.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.67
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $2.23B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters, missing revenue expectations in 5 of those reports.

ALSO REPORTING: ONEOK (OKE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKE]), Innovative Industrial Properties (IIPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IIPR]), Diamondback Energy (FANG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FANG]), Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BBBY]), Ovintiv (OVV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OVV]), Domino's Pizza (DPZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DPZ]), BioMarin Pharmaceutical (BMRN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMRN]), and more.

TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 24

HOME DEPOT (HD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD])

Home Depot (HD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD]) is set to report Q4 earnings before Tuesday’s market open, with analysts expecting roughly a 16% Y/Y decline in profits alongside a ~4% drop in revenue.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating on the stock, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant model remains at Hold, reflecting concerns around growth and valuation.

SA author Justin Purohit also takes a neutral stance [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4872410-home-depot-q4-earnings-preview-housing-trends-in-focus-shares-fairly-valued] ahead of the release, noting that shares have held up relatively well despite softness in the housing market, with management’s outlook for housing demand through 2026 likely to be a key focus. He views the stock as fairly valued in the current environment.

By contrast, contributor Vladimir Dimitrov, CFA, retains a Sell rating [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4865053-home-depot-canary-in-the-coal-mine], arguing that recent weakness in the stock may only be the beginning as margins stay under pressure. He also highlights that valuation remains elevated relative to profitability, while macro risks tied to the housing cycle have intensified lately.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.53
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $38.03B
 	* Earnings Insight: Home Depot has consistently outperformed revenue estimates in the past 6 of the 8 quarters and EPS forecasts in 50% of those reports.

ALSO REPORTING: Realty Income (O [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/O]), Lucid Group (LCID [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LCID]), AMC Entertainment Holdings (AMC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMC]), American Tower (AMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMT]), MercadoLibre (MELI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MELI]), HP (HPQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPQ]), First Solar (FSLR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FSLR]), EOG Resources (EOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EOG]), Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BNS]), Workday (WDAY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDAY]), Tanger Factory Outlet Centers (SKT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SKT]), Clean Energy Fuels (CLNE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLNE]), Mosaic (MOS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MOS]), LTC Properties (LTC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LTC]), Elanco Animal Health (ELAN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ELAN]), Axon Enterprise (AXON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXON]), Range Resources (RRC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RRC]), DigitalOcean (DOCN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCN]), Amer Sports (AS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AS]), CAVA Group (CAVA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAVA]), and more.

WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 25

NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA])

NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]) will headline the week’s earnings, with its Q4 report due Wednesday after the close. Analysts expect revenue and earnings to surge roughly 70% Y/Y, driven by sustained AI-chip demand and continued strength in data-center spending.

Recent developments have kept the company in focus, including a broad multi-year AI infrastructure partnership with Meta [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4552879-nvidia-announces-multi-year-partnership-with-meta-to-expand-ai-infrastructure] Platforms (META [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/META]), which expands deployment of Nvidia’s Grace CPUs, Spectrum-X networking, GB300 systems, and confidential computing tools across Meta’s platforms.

Oppenheimer reiterated [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4553985-nvidia-likely-to-offer-typical-upside-when-it-reports-q4-results-oppenheimer] its Outperform rating and $265 price target on NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]), saying the company could deliver a “typical” $2B–$3B revenue upside in Q4, driven by strong demand for GB300 Ultra systems and rising hyperscaler capex, which is projected to climb to about $650B in 2026 from over $400B in 2025.

Analyst Rick Schafer also pointed to Nvidia’s product roadmap as a key growth lever, with the higher-priced Vera Rubin platform expected to ramp later this year and potentially add around $8B in revenue, while expanding AI workloads, rapid growth in reasoning tokens, and a potential $50B opportunity in China could further lift Nvidia’s total addressable market, now estimated near $4T, reinforcing the view that the company remains the dominant platform in AI infrastructure.

Views on NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]) remain sharply split heading into earnings.

While Wall Street broadly holds a Strong Buy stance, Seeking Alpha’s Quant model is more cautious with a Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA/ratings/quant-ratings] on valuation grounds.

Seeking Alpha contributor Dair Sansyzbayev is firmly bullish, calling Nvidia a Strong Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4872717-nvidia-stock-deep-discount-before-earnings] given its dominance in AI accelerators, accelerating revenue and EPS growth, strong operating leverage, and powerful free-cash-flow generation, with hyperscaler spending expected to drive another blowout quarter.

Julia Ostian takes a more measured stance, rating the stock Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4871478-nvidia-q4-why-even-a-record-beat-could-sink-the-stock]. She acknowledges Nvidia’s unmatched ecosystem, margins, and leadership but warns that expectations for a massive beat are extremely high, with rising inventory and sentiment risk potentially triggering volatility, making post-earnings entry or dollar-cost averaging more prudent.

On the bearish side, Oriental Trader assigns a Strong Sell [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4870585-nvidia-stock-may-be-at-risk-as-big-tech-capex-concerns-mount], arguing Nvidia’s valuation depends heavily on sustained Big Tech AI capex, and any slowdown could expose significant downside given its enormous market cap and still-elevated forward multiple.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.53
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $65.69B
 	* Earnings Insight: Nvidia outperformed revenue and EPS estimates in 8 consecutive quarters.

ALSO REPORTING: Salesforce (CRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM]), Zoom Communications (ZM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZM]), Teladoc Health (TDOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TDOC]), Trade Desk (TTD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TTD]), Snowflake (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]), Lowe's Companies (LOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LOW]), Amarin (AMRN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMRN]), VICI Properties (VICI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VICI]), C3.ai (AI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AI]), Apache (APA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/APA]), EPR Properties (EPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EPR]), Starwood Property Trust (STWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STWD]), TJX Companies (TJX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TJX]), Agilent Technologies (A [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/A]), Bank of Montreal (BMO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMO]), ACADIA Pharmaceuticals (ACAD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACAD]), Magnite (MGNI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MGNI]), Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IONS]), Nutanix (NTNX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTNX]), Geron (GERN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GERN]), Star Bulk Carriers (SBLK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SBLK]), Schrödinger (SDGR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SDGR]), Icahn Enterprises L.P. (IEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IEP]), Pure Storage (PSTG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSTG]), and more.

THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 26

BAIDU (BIDU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU])

Chinese search giant Baidu (BIDU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU]) is set to report Q4 results before Thursday’s open, with analysts expecting profit to decline roughly 47% Y/Y.

The company recently approved a new $5B share repurchase program [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4547623-baidu-announces-5b-buyback-and-dividend-policy] running through December 2028 and introduced a dividend policy that could include both regular and special payouts, signaling a more shareholder-return-focused approach.

While sell-side analysts retain a Buy rating, Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIDU/ratings/quant-ratings].

SA contributor Michael Ngan also rates the stock a Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4871246-baidus-ai-initiatives-are-exciting-what-about-search-and-the-rest], noting Baidu’s core search business continues to face structural pressure from stagnant user growth and declining ARPU. Management is increasingly leaning on AI initiatives to drive the next growth phase, with infrastructure, applications, and its Kunlunxin AI-chip unit gaining traction and potential IPO visibility. However, these newer businesses remain less profitable than search, creating a near-term margin headwind despite improving long-term positioning.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.41
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $4.73B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has topped EPS estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters, exceeding revenue expectations in 5 of those reports.

ALSO REPORTING: Viatris (VTRS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTRS]), Main Street Capital Holdings (MAIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAIN]), TD Bank Group (TD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TD]), Cronos Group (CRON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRON]), Zscaler (ZS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZS]), Dell Technologies (DELL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DELL]), Royal Bank of Canada (RY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RY]), Cheniere Energy (LNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LNG]), Autodesk (ADSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ADSK]), PENN Entertainment (PENN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PENN]), Intuit (INTU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTU]), MannKind (MNKD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MNKD]), Rocket Companies (RKT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RKT]), Opko Health (OPK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OPK]), iQIYI (IQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IQ]), MP Materials (MP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MP]), Sunrun (RUN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RUN]), Contura Energy (CTRA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CTRA]), Pembina Pipeline (PBA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PBA]), Clover Health (CLOV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLOV]), Hormel Foods (HRL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HRL]), Coupang (CPNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CPNG]), Ambarella (AMBA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMBA]), NetApp (NTAP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTAP]), Hertz Global Holdings (HTZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HTZ]), and more.

FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 27

title: ONE Gas Q4 Earnings Beat Estimates, Revenues Increase Y/Y
content:
ONE Gas, Inc. OGS reported fourth quarter 2025 operating earnings per share (EPS) of $1.48, which beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.42 by 4%. In the year-ago quarter, the company reported earnings of $1.35.

OGS reported 2025 adjusted earnings of $4.48 per share compared with $3.94 in 2024, reflecting a year-over-year increase of 13.71%.

OGS’ Revenues

ONE Gas recorded revenues of $689.4 million, which beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $675 million by 2.1%. The top line increased 9.31% from $630.7 million in the prior-year quarter.

OGS reported total revenues of $2.43 billion in 2025 compared with $2.08 billion in 2024, reflecting a year-over-year increase of 16.5%.

ONE Gas, Inc. Price, Consensus and EPS Surprise

ONE Gas, Inc. price-consensus-eps-surprise-chart | ONE Gas, Inc. Quote

Highlights of OGS’ Earnings Release

Total natural gas volumes delivered were 103.7 billion cubic feet, up 1.27% on a year-over-year basis. OGS served 2,290,000 customers, increased 0.57% year over year.

Total operating expenses were $257.7 million, up 6.22% from $242.7 million recorded in the year-ago quarter. The increase in expenses was due to a rise in operations and maintenance expenses, depreciation and amortization expenses, as well as general taxes.

Operating income totaled $139.7 million, up 12.41% from $124.3 million recorded in the year-ago quarter.

OGS incurred net interest expenses of $36.5 million, down 8.30% from $39.8 million recorded in the year-ago quarter.

OGS’ Financial Highlights

As of Dec. 31, 2025, OGS had cash and cash equivalents of $10.6 million compared with $58 million as of Dec. 31, 2024.

Total long-term debt (excluding current maturities) was $2.36 billion as of Dec. 31, 2025, compared with $2.39 billion as of Dec. 31, 2024.

Cash provided by operating activities during 2025 was $578.8 million compared with $368.4 million in the year-ago period.

Capital expenditure for the year totaled $707.2 million compared with $703.2 million in 2024.

OGS’ 2026 Guidance

OGS expects its 2026 adjusted net income to be in the range of $306-$314 million.

The company projects 2026 adjusted earnings to be in the range of $4.83 to $4.95 per share. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for earnings is pegged at $4.71, which is below the company’s guidance range. 

ONE Gas projects its long term adjusted net income to grow by 7-9% and adjusted net income per diluted share growth of 5-7%, in its five-year financial plan.

In 2026, OGS plans to make capital investments, including asset removal costs, of $800 million to maintain its system integrity and its replacement projects.

title: Clean Energy Fuels Corp. (NASDAQ:CLNE) Shares Could Be 37% Below Their Intrinsic Value Estimate
content:
Key Insights

The projected fair value for Clean Energy Fuels is US$4.33 based on 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity Current share price of US$2.72 suggests Clean Energy Fuels is potentially 37% undervalued Analyst price target for CLNE is US$4.71, which is 8.6% above our fair value estimate

Today we will run through one way of estimating the intrinsic value of Clean Energy Fuels Corp. (NASDAQ:CLNE) by taking the expected future cash flows and discounting them to today's value. We will use the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model on this occasion. Believe it or not, it's not too difficult to follow, as you'll see from our example!

Remember though, that there are many ways to estimate a company's value, and a DCF is just one method. If you want to learn more about discounted cash flow, the rationale behind this calculation can be read in detail in the Simply Wall St analysis model.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

The Method

We use what is known as a 2-stage model, which simply means we have two different periods of growth rates for the company's cash flows. Generally the first stage is higher growth, and the second stage is a lower growth phase. In the first stage we need to estimate the cash flows to the business over the next ten years. Where possible we use analyst estimates, but when these aren't available we extrapolate the previous free cash flow (FCF) from the last estimate or reported value. We assume companies with shrinking free cash flow will slow their rate of shrinkage, and that companies with growing free cash flow will see their growth rate slow, over this period. We do this to reflect that growth tends to slow more in the early years than it does in later years.

Generally we assume that a dollar today is more valuable than a dollar in the future, so we need to discount the sum of these future cash flows to arrive at a present value estimate:

10-year free cash flow (FCF) forecast

2026 2027 2028 2029 2030 2031 2032 2033 2034 2035 Levered FCF ($, Millions)  -US$16.8m US$20.7m US$26.6m US$32.2m US$37.3m US$41.8m US$45.7m US$49.2m US$52.3m US$55.2m Growth Rate Estimate Source Analyst x2 Analyst x1 Est @ 28.56% Est @ 21.02% Est @ 15.74% Est @ 12.04% Est @ 9.45% Est @ 7.64% Est @ 6.37% Est @ 5.48% Present Value ($, Millions) Discounted @ 7.3%  -US$15.7 US$18.0 US$21.6 US$24.3 US$26.3 US$27.4 US$28.0 US$28.1 US$27.9 US$27.4

("Est" = FCF growth rate estimated by Simply Wall St)
Present Value of 10-year Cash Flow (PVCF) = US$213m

繼續閱讀

The second stage is also known as Terminal Value, this is the business's cash flow after the first stage. The Gordon Growth formula is used to calculate Terminal Value at a future annual growth rate equal to the 5-year average of the 10-year government bond yield of 3.4%. We discount the terminal cash flows to today's value at a cost of equity of 7.3%.

Terminal Value (TV)= FCF2035 × (1 + g) ÷ (r – g) = US$55m× (1 + 3.4%) ÷ (7.3%– 3.4%) = US$1.5b

Present Value of Terminal Value (PVTV)= TV / (1 + r)10= US$1.5b÷ ( 1 + 7.3%)10= US$737m

The total value is the sum of cash flows for the next ten years plus the discounted terminal value, which results in the Total Equity Value, which in this case is US$950m. To get the intrinsic value per share, we divide this by the total number of shares outstanding. Compared to the current share price of US$2.7, the company appears quite undervalued at a 37% discount to where the stock price trades currently. Remember though, that this is just an approximate valuation, and like any complex formula - garbage in, garbage out.NasdaqGS:CLNE Discounted Cash Flow February 10th 2026

The Assumptions

The calculation above is very dependent on two assumptions. The first is the discount rate and the other is the cash flows. Part of investing is coming up with your own evaluation of a company's future performance, so try the calculation yourself and check your own assumptions. The DCF also does not consider the possible cyclicality of an industry, or a company's future capital requirements, so it does not give a full picture of a company's potential performance. Given that we are looking at Clean Energy Fuels as potential shareholders, the cost of equity is used as the discount rate, rather than the cost of capital (or weighted average cost of capital, WACC) which accounts for debt. In this calculation we've used 7.3%, which is based on a levered beta of 0.862. Beta is a measure of a stock's volatility, compared to the market as a whole. We get our beta from the industry average beta of globally comparable companies, with an imposed limit between 0.8 and 2.0, which is a reasonable range for a stable business.

Check out our latest analysis for Clean Energy Fuels

Looking Ahead:

Although the valuation of a company is important, it shouldn't be the only metric you look at when researching a company. The DCF model is not a perfect stock valuation tool. Rather it should be seen as a guide to "what assumptions need to be true for this stock to be under/overvalued?" If a company grows at a different rate, or if its cost of equity or risk free rate changes sharply, the output can look very different. What is the reason for the share price sitting below the intrinsic value? For Clean Energy Fuels, we've compiled three pertinent aspects you should further examine:

Financial Health: Does CLNE have a healthy balance sheet? Take a look at our free balance sheet analysis with six simple checks on key factors like leverage and risk. Future Earnings: How does CLNE's growth rate compare to its peers and the wider market? Dig deeper into the analyst consensus number for the upcoming years by interacting with our free analyst growth expectation chart. Other Solid Businesses: Low debt, high returns on equity and good past performance are fundamental to a strong business. Why not explore our interactive list of stocks with solid business fundamentals to see if there are other companies you may not have considered!

(Nasdaq: CLNE) announced today it will release financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on February 24, 2026 after market close, followed by an investor conference call at 4:30 p.m. Eastern time (1:30 p.m. Pacific time). Investors interested in participating in the live call can dial 1.800.267.6316 from the U.S. (Conference ID: CLEAN) and international callers can dial 1.203.518.9783. (Conference ID: CLEAN). A telephone replay will be available approximately three hours after the call concludes through March 24, 2026 by dialing 1.844.512.2921 from the U.S., or 1.412.317.6671 from international locations, and entering Replay Pin Number 11160853.

There also will be a simultaneous, live webcast available on the Investor Relations section of the Company's web site at www.cleanenergyfuels.com, which will be available for replay for 30 days.

About Clean Energy Fuels Corp.

Clean Energy Fuels Corp. is the country’s largest provider of the cleanest fuel for the transportation market. Our mission is to decarbonize transportation through the development and delivery of renewable natural gas (RNG), a sustainable fuel derived by capturing methane from organic waste. Clean Energy allows thousands of vehicles, from airport shuttles to city buses to waste and heavy-duty trucks, to reduce their amount of climate-harming greenhouse gas. We operate a vast network of fueling stations across the U.S. and Canada as well as RNG production facilities at dairy farms. Visit www.cleanenergyfuels.com and follow @ce_renewables on X.
